sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 5 fears in the way but 5 take the case of a monk who reflects thus i am now young a mere youth blackhead and blessed with the beauty of youth the heyday of youth the prime of youth but time will come when all age shall touch this body and when grown old and overcome by age not easy is it to turn to the buddha's word not easy things are forest wildernesses the outland bed and sea to seek before that state come to me unwelcome undesired unloved no i will put forth energy against that time even to attain the unattained to master the unmastered to realize the unrealized and of that state possessed i will dwell comforted even when all monks this is the first fear in the way again he reflects i have health and well-being a good digestion disease which is neither over cold nor overheated but even and suitable for striving but time will be when sickness shall touch this body and sick and ill not easy is it to turn to the buddha's word no i will put forth energy [Music] and well comforted even when sick monks this is the second fear in the way again he reflects now there is no famine and crops are good food is easy to get and it is easy to keep oneself going by cleanings and favors but time will be when there is a famine bad crops and difficulty in getting food now will it be easy to keep oneself going by gleanings and favors and the famine-stricken men will move to where there is ample food and there 1 will dwell in a crowd and a throng and where such conditions are not easy is it to turn the to the buddha's word lo i will put forth energy and dwell comforted even in time of famine monks this is a third fear in the way again he reflects now men dwell in friendly fellowship together as mingle milk and water they do not quarrel but look upon 1 another with friendly eye but time will be when fear is about perils or bandits and the country folk mound their cats and drive away and the fear-stricken men will move away to where there is safety and there 1 will live in crowds and strongs and where such conditions are not easy is it to turn to the buddha's word no i will put forth energy and well comforted even in time of fear this is the fourth fear in the way moreover monks the monk reflects thus now the sangha lives in friendly fellowship together finding comfort in 1 teaching but the time will come when the sangha will be divided and when that happens not easy is it to turn to the buddha's word not easy things are forest wildernesses the outland bed and seed to seek before that state come and welcome undesired unloved no i will put forth energy against that time even to attain the unattained to master the unmastered to realize the unrealized and of that state possessed i would dwell comforted even though the sangha be divided monks this is the fifth year in the way these monks are the 5 years in the way that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so this suta is reflections on impermanence because of impermanence our present statement can always change so the person cultivating the holy life or the spiritual path should always always consider that you you are growing all day by day and when we grow older it's much more difficult to practice the spiritual path so now it's better that we put forth energy now than to think of putting energy putting forth energy in the future uh as we heard the previous uttar that whenever we can find some time every day we should practice some chatos samatha to tranquilize the mind otherwise we are not living by dhamma the second 1 is to consider that sickness 1 day will come to us so before we become signal we should put fourth energy the third 1 feminine fourth 1 bandits and the fifth 1 which is applicable only to monks is that the sangha will be divided so these are the contemplations of impermanence that we should often make so that we get the urgency to practice the holy life the next 1 is sutra
